content.problemStatement:
kkk points (x1,y1),(x2,y2),‚Ä¶,(xk,yk)(x_1,y_1) , (x_2,y_2), \ldots, (x_k, y_k)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Äč),(x2‚Äč,y2‚Äč),‚Ä¶,(xk‚Äč,yk‚Äč) are said to **belong** to a line of slope mmm within tolerance DDD, if there exists a line with slope mmm that's at most **vertical** distance DDD away from every point.

Formally, there should exist kkk other points (x1,y1‚Ä≤),(x2,y2‚Ä≤)...(xk,yk‚Ä≤)(x_1,y'_1) , (x_2,y'_2)...(x_k, y'_k)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Ä≤‚Äč),(x2‚Äč,y2‚Ä≤‚Äč)...(xk‚Äč,yk‚Ä≤‚Äč) such that:


‚ą£yi‚ąíyi‚Ä≤‚ą£‚Č§D|y_i - y'_i| \leq D‚ą£yi‚Äč‚ąíyi‚Ä≤‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§D for every iii; and
yi‚Ä≤=yj‚Ä≤+(xi‚ąíxj)‚čÖmy'_i = y'_j + (x_i-x_j) \cdot myi‚Ä≤‚Äč=yj‚Ä≤‚Äč+(xi‚Äč‚ąíxj‚Äč)‚čÖm for each 1‚Č§i,j‚Č§k1 \leq i, j \leq k1‚Č§i,j‚Č§k

Note that this just means the points (x1,y1‚Ä≤),(x2,y2‚Ä≤)...(xk,yk‚Ä≤)(x_1,y'_1) , (x_2,y'_2)...(x_k, y'_k)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Ä≤‚Äč),(x2‚Äč,y2‚Ä≤‚Äč)...(xk‚Äč,yk‚Ä≤‚Äč) all lie on a single line of slope mmm.
You are given NNN points (x1,y1),(x2,y2),‚Ä¶,(xN,yN)(x_1,y_1) , (x_2,y_2), \ldots ,(x_N, y_N)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Äč),(x2‚Äč,y2‚Äč),‚Ä¶,(xN‚Äč,yN‚Äč) and a tolerance value DDD.

Answer QQQ queries of the following form:

Given L,R, and KL, R, \text{ and } KL,R, and K, find the number of **integer** slopes in [L,R][L, R][L,R], for which **at least** KKK points belong to a line of this slope within tolerance DDD.

That is, find the number of integers mmm satisfying:


L‚Č§m‚Č§RL \leq m \leq RL‚Č§m‚Č§R
At least KKK of the input points **belong** to a line of slope mmm, within tolerance DDD.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,DN, DN,D and QQQ ‚ÄĒ the number of points, tolerance value and number of queries, respectively.
The next NNN lines describe the points. The iii-th of these NNN lines contains 2 space-separated integers xix_ixi‚Äč and yiy_iyi‚Äč
The next QQQ lines describe the queries. The iii-th of these QQQ lines contains 3 space-separated integers Li,RiL_i, R_iLi‚Äč,Ri‚Äč and KiK_iKi‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the parameters for the iii-th query.




Output Format
For each testcase, output QQQ lines. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines should contain the answer to the ithi^{th}ith query: the number of integer slopes in the range [Li,Ri][L_i, R_i][Li‚Äč,Ri‚Äč] for which there exists a line with at least KiK_iKi‚Äč points **belonging** to it within tolerance DDD.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500500500.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For slope 000, a line exists such that all 333 points belong to it within a tolerance of 111.  However, for slope =1= 1=1, at most 222 points can belong to a line within a tolerance of 111.

For the first query, K=2K = 2K=2. Both 0 and 1 will satisfy the conditions. Therefore ans=2\text{ans} = 2ans=2.
In the second query, K=3K = 3K=3, and only slope = 0 will satisfy the condition. Therefore ans=1\text{ans} = 1ans=1.
